problem_statement: Given a list of integers, return a new list where each element is replaced by the product of all other elements (without using division).
solution_source_code:
def product_except_self(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    result = [1] * n
    left = 1
    for i in range(n):
        result[i] = left
        left *= nums[i]
    right = 1
    for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        result[i] *= right
        right *= nums[i]
    return result
correct_explanation: At each index i, this line stores the product of all elements to the left of i. By the end of the first pass, result[i] holds the left product prefix. The second pass will then multiply in the right product, giving us the full product of all other elements without division.

problem_statement: Implement a function to find the longest common prefix among a list of strings.
correct_explanation: This loop progressively trims the candidate prefix until the current string starts with it. By iteratively shortening the prefix one character at a time from the right, we find the longest string that is a valid prefix for every string seen so far.

problem_statement: Write a function to check whether a given string is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards).
correct_explanation: This line initializes two pointers at opposite ends of the string. The two-pointer technique allows us to compare characters from the outside in without reversing the string or using extra space, resulting in an O(n) time and O(1) space solution.

problem_statement: Given an array of integers, find two numbers that add up to a target sum and return their indices.
correct_explanation: This line computes the value that, when added to nums[i], equals the target. By subtracting the current element from the target we get the complement — the exact number we need to have already stored in the HashMap. If it is present we can return immediately, giving an O(n) solution instead of the O(n²) nested-loop alternative.
